title: Determine the domain of functions
description: Determine the domain of a function according to the algebraic limitations of that function.
topic_path: Determining the domain of a function

hints:
$f$ is a [[☃ definition 1]].

Rational functions are defined for all real numbers except those which result in a denominator that is equal to zero (i.e., division by zero).

The denominator of $f(x)=\dfrac{x-3}{x+2}$ is $x+2$. We want that to not be equal to zero:

$\begin{align}
x+2&\neq 0
\\\\
x&\neq -2
\end{align}$

The domain of $f$ is all real values of $x$ such that $x\neq -2$.

We can also see this is true if we graph the function. If you don't have a graphing calculator, here's a graph we made.

[[☃ image 1]]

You can see the graph is defined everywhere except for $x=-2$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq -2$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq -2$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 3$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\leq 3$
All real values of $x$

hints:
$g$ is a [[☃ definition 1]].

Rational functions are defined for all real numbers except those which result in a denominator that is equal to zero (i.e., division by zero).

The denominator of $g(x)=\dfrac{x+6}{2}$ is $2$. We want that to not be equal to zero, but that is true regardless of the value of $x$. In other words, there is no $x$-value that would make the denominator equal to zero.
The domain of $g$ is all real values of $x$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq -6$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq -6$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 2$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\leq 2$
All real values of $x$

hints:
$h$ is a [[☃ definition 1]].

Rational functions are defined for all real numbers except those which result in a denominator that is equal to zero (i.e., division by zero).

The denominator of $h(x)=\dfrac{4}{x-5}$ is $x-5$. We want that to not be equal to zero:

$\begin{align}
x-5&\neq 0
\\\\
x&\neq 5
\end{align}$
The domain of $h$ is all real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 5$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 4$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 4$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 5$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\leq 5$
All real values of $x$

hints:
$f$ is a [[☃ definition 1]].

Rational functions are defined for all real numbers except those which result in a denominator that is equal to zero (i.e., division by zero).

The denominator of $f(x)=\dfrac{x}{x-7}$ is $x-7$. We want that to not be equal to zero:

$\begin{align}
x-7&\neq 0
\\\\
x&\neq 7
\end{align}$
The domain of $f$ is all real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 7$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 0$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 0$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 7$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\leq 7$
All real values of $x$

hints:
$g$ is a *square root function*.

Square root functions are defined for all real numbers except those which result in a negative expression below the square root.

The expression below the square root in $g(x)=\sqrt{8x}$ is $8x$. We want that to be greater than or equal to zero:

$\begin{align}
8x&\geq 0
\\\\
x&\geq 0
\end{align}$
The domain of $g$ is all real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 0$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 0$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 0$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq \dfrac18$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq \dfrac18$
All real values of $x$

hints:
$h$ is a *square root function*.

Square root functions are defined for all real numbers except those which result in a negative expression below the square root.

The expression below the square root in $h(x)=\sqrt{x-10}$ is $x-10$. We want that to be greater than or equal to zero:

$\begin{align}
x-10&\geq 0
\\\\
x&\geq 10
\end{align}$
The domain of $h$ is all real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 10$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 0$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 10$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 10$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 0$
All real values of $x$

hints:
$f$ is a *square root function*.

Square root functions are defined for all real numbers except those which result in a negative expression below the square root.

The expression below the square root in $f(x)=\sqrt{-2x}$ is $-2x$. We want that to be greater than or equal to zero:

$\begin{align}
-2x&\geq 0
\\\\
x&\leq 0
\end{align}$

*Notice that the inequality sign changed its direction because we divided by $-2$ which is negative.*
The domain of $f$ is all real values of $x$ such that $x\leq 0$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x= 0$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 0$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\leq 0$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 0$
All real values of $x$

hints:
$g$ is a *square root function*.

Square root functions are defined for all real numbers except those which result in a negative expression below the square root.

The expression below the square root in $g(x)=\sqrt{x+3}$ is $x+3$. We want that to be greater than or equal to zero:

$\begin{align}
x+3&\geq 0
\\\\
x&\geq -3
\end{align}$
The domain of $g$ is all real values of $x$ such that $x\geq -3$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq -3$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq -3$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 3$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 3$
All real values of $x$

hints:
$h$ is a *linear function*.

Is there an input value that would make a linear expression undefined?
There isn't! Linear functions are defined for all real numbers.
The domain of $h$ is all real values of $x$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 4$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 4$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 5$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 5$
All real values of $x$

hints:
$f$ is a *quadratic function*.

Is there an input value that would make a quadratic expression undefined?
There isn't! Quadratic functions are defined for all real numbers.
The domain of $f$ is all real values of $x$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq -1$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq -1$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 0$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 0$
All real values of $x$

hints:
$g$ is a *linear function*.

Is there an input value that would make a linear expression undefined?
There isn't! Linear functions are defined for all real numbers.
The domain of $g$ is all real values of $x$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 0$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 0$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq \dfrac52$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq \dfrac52$
All real values of $x$

hints:
$h$ is a *quadratic function*.

Is there an input value that would make a quadratic expression undefined?
There isn't! Quadratic functions are defined for all real numbers.
The domain of $h$ is all real values of $x$.

choices.content: All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 3$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 3$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\neq 4$
All real values of $x$ such that $x\geq 4$
